# Copilot Coding Agent — repository instructions

Overview
- These instructions configure how the Copilot coding agent should operate in this repository. The goal: safe, helpful, auditable contributions.

Enablement checklist (owner actions)
- Ensure the Copilot Coding Agent app is installed and has the minimal required scopes.
- Add agent owner(s) (@joe10832) and a fallback reviewer team (e.g., @Munyay/maintainers).
- Protect main with branch protection rules requiring CI and at least one review.
- Ensure repository secrets and CI tokens are configured and never exposed to agents.

Branch & PR behavior
- Branch prefix: copilot/
- Default PR title prefix: "[WIP] Copilot:"
- PR body must include:
  - "Generated by Copilot" disclaimer
  - Original prompt used (or a short summary)
  - List of files changed
  - Checklist:
    - [ ] All CI checks passed
    - [ ] Manual review requested from @joe10832 or maintainers
- Label agent PRs automatically with: agent/generated, needs-review

Commit messages
- Format: "copilot: <short summary> — generated"
- Include a reference to the prompt in the PR body (not commit message) to avoid leaking prompt details in commit metadata.

Approval & merge policy
- Do not enable auto-merge for Copilot PRs unless explicitly approved by a repo owner.
- Require at least one human code review and passing CI before merge.
- For non-code changes (docs, README, templates), one review by a maintainer is sufficient.

Security & sensitive operations
- Copilot must not:
  - Create or update release credentials, deploy keys, or secrets
  - Publish packages or perform any monetary transaction
  - Modify billing or marketplace settings
- Any PR that touches .github/workflows, CI config, or infra must be flagged "security/infra" and require two human reviewers.

Testing & verification
- Copilot PRs must trigger full CI:
  - Lint → Unit Tests → Integration Tests (if configured)
- If the PR adds or changes tests, the agent must run them locally via CI before opening the PR.

Prompt templates (store under .github/agents/prompts/)
- pr_summary.md.template
  - "Short summary (one sentence):\nWhat changed:\nWhy:\nTesting performed:\nManual steps required:\n"
- commit_template.md
  - "copilot: <one-line summary> — generated"

Audit trail & transparency
- PR body should include:
  - Generated-by tag
  - Link to the prompt file used from .github/agents/prompts/
  - Agent version (if available)
- Keep a small log in .github/agents/agent-log.md for major agent-driven changes (date, author, summary).

Failure & revert policy
- If an agent PR causes regression after merge:
  - Revert immediately and open an incident issue with label incident/agent
  - Agent owner investigates and disables or adjusts the agent until root cause is resolved

Best practices for maintainers
- Review agent PRs promptly—agents accelerate change but need human oversight.
- Keep prompts small and modular; avoid embedding secrets or large examples.
- Iterate on agent prompts based on the PR review feedback and update the template files.

Contact & support
- Maintainer: @joe10832
- For questions about agent configuration, open an issue with label: agent/config
